nodes.title: Import only the template dependencies
nodes.why: The standalone component needs reactive-form directives and HttpClient, while Angular built-in control flow does not require CommonModule.
nodes.alternative: Keep CommonModule in every standalone component even when the template does not use its directives or pipes.
nodes.tradeoff: Minimal imports make dependencies explicit, but a later template pipe or directive must be added intentionally.

nodes.title: Expose validation before returning
nodes.why: Marking controls touched ensures invalid fields reveal feedback before submission exits early.
nodes.alternative: Return immediately on an invalid form and leave untouched fields without visible guidance.
nodes.tradeoff: Showing every current error is clearer on submit, though quieter validation can be preferable while the user is still typing.

nodes.title: Own success and error state around the request
nodes.why: The component must reset loading state in both request outcomes and only clear the form after success.
nodes.alternative: Set a success message before the request resolves or leave isSubmitting true after an error.
nodes.tradeoff: Explicit handlers duplicate a small amount of cleanup, but they keep the UI state deterministic.
